proposition: Anne-Marie Slaughter is the Director of Policy Planning at the U.S. Department of State.
Anne-Marie Slaughter's office phone number is (202) 647-2972.
The U.S. Department of State has bureaus CCO, INL, DRL, GTIP, S/WCI, and PRM focused on protecting individuals from violence, protecting rights through law, and meeting humanitarian needs.
The strategy to integrate bureaus protecting individuals signifies a major policy direction of the U.S. Department of State.
Anne-Marie Slaughter personally initiated the integration of bureaus protecting individuals at the Department of State.
One of the strongest impressions from the QDDR is the consolidation of bureaus focusing on protecting individuals.
The consolidated bureaus focus on protecting individuals from insurgent or criminal violence, protecting their rights through law, and meeting their basic needs in humanitarian emergencies.
The bureaus focusing on protection from insurgent or criminal violence include CCO and INL.
The bureaus focusing on protecting rights through law include DRL, GTIP, and S/WCI.
The bureau focusing on meeting basic needs in humanitarian emergencies is PRM.
The consolidation of these bureaus represents a major statement about the direction that the world is going and must follow.
Anne-Marie Slaughter is not the Director of Policy Planning at the U.S. Department of State.
Anne-Marie Slaughter's office phone number is (202) 647-2973.
The U.S. Department of State does not have bureaus CCO, INL, DRL, GTIP, S/WCI, and PRM focused on protecting individuals from violence, protecting rights through law, and meeting humanitarian needs.
The strategy to integrate bureaus protecting individuals does not signify a major policy direction of the U.S. Department of State.
Anne‑Marie Slaughter never originated the integration of bureaus protecting individuals at the Department of State.
One of the strongest impressions from the QDDR is NOT the consolidation of bureaus focusing on protecting individuals.

The consolidated bureaus no longer focus on protecting individuals from insurgent or criminal violence, nor do they protect their rights through law or meet their basic needs in humanitarian emergencies.
The bureaus focusing on protection from insurgent or criminal violence do not include CCO and INL.
The bureaus focusing on protecting rights through law exclude DRL, GTIP, and S/WCI.
The bureau focusing on meeting basic needs in humanitarian emergencies is not PRM.
The consolidation of these bureaus does not represent a major statement about the direction that the world is going and must follow.
